sections.paragraph:
προσφωνέω impf. προσεφώνουν; 1 aor. προσεφώνησα; aor. pass. inf. προσεφωνηθῆναι 1 Esdr. 6:6 (Hom. et al.; ins, pap, LXX; EpArist 306; Joseph.).

① to call out or speak to, call out, address (Hom. et al., as a rule w. acc. of pers.) w. dat. of pers. (Diod S 4, 48, 1; Diog. L. 7, 7; PPetr II, 38b, 3 [242 b.c.]; PTebt 27, 109; Mitt-Wilck I/2, 27 verso, 15.—Cp. Eus., HE 4, 3 [introd. to Qua.]) Mt 11:16; Lk 7:32; 23:20. τῇ Ἑβραΐδι διαλέκτῳ προσεφώνει αὐτοῖς Ac 22:2; without dat. of pers., 21:40; 22:2 D. Likew. abs. Lk 23:20 v.l.

② to call to oneself, call to, summon, with implication of shared interests τινά someone (Jos., Ant. 7, 156) Lk 6:13 (D ἐφώνησεν); 13:12; Ac 11:2 D.—DELG s.v. φωνή. M-M.

impf. impf. = imperfect

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

inf. inf. = infinitive

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

pers. pers. = person(s)

dat. dat. = dative

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

introd. introd. = introduction

Qua Qua = Quadratus fragment, II a.d.—List 1

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

abs. abs. = absolute

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 887
